w:r.w:t: DSA4264: Sense-making Case Analysis: Public Policy and Society
Group Proj
ect
Grading Rubric
(Presentation)
Grade
Criterion 1:
Insightfulness
Criterion 2:
Communication
Excellent (A)
Insights are highly relevant to the
problem
and demonstrate exceptional understanding
of the key stakeholder and their needs
 Analysis goes beyond surface-level observations, revealing deep, thoughtful consideration
of the data, approach, and limitations
 Insights have clear, practical applications and add significant value to the
stakeholder
Interactive demo is helpful for stakeholders to understand and act on the key insights of the project
 Presentation is
very
clear, logical, and well-structured
 Complex ideas are explained concisely and precisely, without unnecessary jargon
 Visual aids
(like charts)
are highly effective, enhancing understanding of the content
 Delivery is engaging, maintaining audience interest throughout
Satisfactory (B)
 Insights are generally relevant to the
problem
and show good understanding
of the key stakeholder’s concerns
 Analysis shows depth in some areas but may remain surface-level in others
, and may miss out on a few critical points
 Insights have some practical applications, adding moderate value to the
stakeholder
 Presentation is generally clear and well-structured, with some minor logical gaps
 Most ideas are explained clearly, but some complex points may lack precision
 Visual aids are helpful but may not always enhance understanding
 Delivery is mostly engaging
Needs Improvement (C)
 Insights have limited relevance to the project topic and demonstrate basic understanding
 Analysis remains largely surface-level, lacking depth and thoughtful consideration
 Insights have minimal practical applications and add little value to the project
 Presentation lacks clear structure and logical flow
 Ideas are often unclear or imprecisely explained, with overuse of jargon
 Visual aids (if used) are ineffective or distracting
 Delivery is unengaging, failing to maintain audience interest
DSA4264: Sense-making Case Analysis: Public Policy and Society
Group Project
Grading Rubric (
Technical Report
Grade
Criterion 1:
Robustness
Criterion 2:
Execution
Criterion 3:
Communication
Excellent (A)
 Demonstrates exceptional application of advanced data science techniques with deep understanding of their theoretical foundations and practical implications in the public sector context
 Approach is robust, accounting for various scenarios including data quality issues, policy changes, and ethical considerations
 Detailed explanation of each step in the methodology, including rationale for choices made in the context of public sector constraints
 Clear consideration of limitations, potential biases, and areas for future improvement relevant to the public sector context
 Thorough addressing of key stakeholders' concerns, with clear links between methodology and policy implications
 Code is highly efficient, well-organi
s
ed, and follows best practices for th
e chosen language (either Python or R)
 Extensive use of comments and documentation, making the code easily understandable
 Artifacts (e.g.
charts,
diagrams,
notebooks
) are of exceptional quality and significantly enhance
the key stakeholders’
understanding and usability
 Clear demonstration of how the code and artifacts directly address project objectives
 Technical report is
very
clear
and concise
, making it easy for technical audiences to get up to speed quickly
 Complex technical concepts are explained precisely, accessible to both technical and non-technical audiences
 Effective use of visual aids (diagrams, charts, etc.) to enhance understanding
 Consistent and appropriate use of technical terminology
 Logical flow of ideas, with clear connections between sections
No problems with
grammar, spelling, and formatting
Satisfactory (B)

 Applies appropriate data science techniques with good understanding, using a suitable combination of methods for the public sector problem, but may not fully exploit advanced techniques where applicable
 Approach is mostly robust, addressing major public sector considerations but may miss some edge cases
 Most steps in the methodology are explained, with some rationale provided in the public sector context
 Some consideration of limitations and biases, but discussion of public sector-specific improvements may be limited
 Addresses most key stakeholders' concerns, but some connections between methodology and policy implications may be unclear
 Code is generally efficient and organized, with some adherence to best practices
 Adequate use of comments and documentation, though some areas may lack clarity
 Artifacts are of good quality and generally helpful to
the key stakeholder
, with some room for improvement
 Most aspects of the code and artifacts align with project objectives
 Technical report is generally clear and
concise
, with minor issues
 Most technical concepts are explained well, but some may be too complex or oversimplified
 Some use of visual aids, though not always effectively integrated
 Mostly consistent use of technical terminology, with occasional lapses
 Generally logical flow of ideas, though some connections may be unclear
A few
grammatical, spelling, or formatting errors
Needs Improvement (C)
 Applies basic data science techniques with limited sophistication, relying on a narrow range of methods that may not be fully appropriate for the complexity of public sector problems
 Methodology shows basic awareness of public sector issues but lacks depth or comprehensive thinking
 Approach has significant gaps in robustness, failing to account for many public sector-specific scenarios
 Explanation of methodology is superficial, with little rationale for choices made in the public sector context
 Little to no consideration of public sector-specific limitations, biases, or potential improvements
 Inadequate addressing of key stakeholders' concerns, with weak links between methodology and policy implications
 Code is functional but inefficient, poorly organized, or fails to follow best practices
 Minimal use of comments and documentation, making the code difficult to understand
 Artifacts are of low quality or provide little value to
the key stakeholder
 Unclear how some aspects of the code and artifacts relate to project objectives
 Technical report lacks clarity and
is overly verbose
, making it difficult to follow
 Technical concepts are poorly explained, either too complex or overly simplified
 Minimal or ineffective use of visual aids
 Inconsistent or inappropriate use of technical terminology
 Disjointed flow of ideas, with weak connections between sections
 Numerous grammatical, spelling, or formatting errors
